sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Corteva (CTVA) to Release Quarterly Earnings on Tuesday
feed.summary: Corteva (CTVA) is anticipated to release its Q4 2025 earnings after market close on Tuesday, February 3rd, with analysts forecasting earnings of $0.22 per share and revenue of $4.2281 billion. The company last reported earnings on November 4th, beating estimates with ($0.23) EPS and revenue of $2.62 billion, a 6.5% increase year-over-year. Corteva has also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.18, and analysts currently rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy" with a consensus target price of $79.47.

feed.title: Envestnet Portfolio Solutions Inc. Has $2.35 Million Stake in Corteva, Inc. $CTVA
feed.summary: Envestnet Portfolio Solutions Inc. has reduced its stake in Corteva, Inc. by 36.7% in Q3, now holding 34,667 shares valued at approximately $2.35 million. Despite this, other institutional investors have increased their holdings, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating for Corteva with a target price of $79.47. Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) recently surpassed earnings and revenue estimates and offers a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Gartner: Humanoid Robots Just a Supply Chain 'Blip'
feed.summary: Gartner predicts that humanoid robots will not significantly penetrate supply chains by 2028 due to their high costs, technical immaturity, and limited adaptability. The firm suggests that most companies will not move beyond pilot programs, with deployments restricted to controlled environments. Instead, Gartner highlights polyfunctional robots as a more efficient and cost-effective alternative for warehouse and distribution center operations.

feed.title: Envestnet Portfolio Solutions Inc. Has $2.53 Million Stock Holdings in Adobe Inc. $ADBE
feed.summary: Envestnet Portfolio Solutions Inc. significantly reduced its stake in Adobe Inc. by 70.8% in Q3, selling 17,326 shares but still holding 7,158 shares valued at approximately $2.525 million. Adobe reported strong Q3 results, beating EPS and revenue expectations, and provided optimistic guidance for Q1 and full-year 2026. Analyst sentiment for Adobe is mixed, with a consensus "Hold" rating and an average price target of $397.19, reflecting both recent downgrades and continued "Buy/Outperform" ratings from various firms.

feed.title: Apple Inc. $AAPL Holdings Lessened by First Merchants Corp

Despite this reduction, Apple reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates with EPS of $1.85 and revenue of $102.47 billion. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for Apple, with a consensus price target of $282.80, citing strong demand, growth in India, and new product/monetization catalysts.

Technically, LRCX is in a strong upward trend, outperforming the market and its peers, aligning with a strategy that combines basic strength with price trend strength.

feed.title: (TMFG) Volatility Zones as Tactical Triggers

feed.title: Aletheia Maintains Sell on Apple (AAPL), Lowers Target Price
feed.summary: Aletheia Capital has maintained a Sell rating on Apple (AAPL) and lowered its price target to $205 from $215. The firm cites pressure on Apple's hardware margins due to rising memory costs and increased R&D investments in AI, despite robust demand for the iPhone 17. The report suggests that iPhone 17's Bill of Materials will significantly increase, leading to a projected decline in gross margins.

Insider selling has also been reported, with 62,223 shares sold in the last 90 days, though Oracle is benefiting from positive catalysts like its TikTok JV, OpenAI collaboration, and a large AI-related backlog, counterbalanced by risks such as AI-sentiment volatility and potential legal action. Oracle reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates, and analysts currently rate the stock as a "Moderate Buy."

feed.title: Navy asks Northrop Grumman to redesign avionics displays plagued by obsolescence in $11.5 million deal
feed.summary: The U.S. Navy has awarded Northrop Grumman an $11.5 million deal to redesign 82 avionics displays for its E-2D Advanced Hawkeye surveillance aircraft, including 67 for the U.S. Navy and 15 for Japan's E-2D modernization. This initiative, part of the Obsolescence Redesign Weapons Displays (ORWD) program, aims to address the issue of obsolete electronic parts by replacing them with modern components while maintaining the original form, fit, and function to avoid costly full-system redesigns. The upgraded displays will be ruggedized, sunlight-readable, night-vision-goggles-compatible, and feature multitouch interfaces to enhance situational awareness.

feed.title: Prediction: These 2 Top Stocks Will Be the Largest REIT Dividend Payers in 2026
feed.summary: The article predicts that Prologis (PLD) and American Tower (AMT) will be the largest REIT dividend payers in 2026, building on their lead from the previous year. Both companies are expected to increase their dividend payments by about 5% due to strong financial performance, comfortable payout ratios, and strengthened balance sheets. Prologis's core FFO per share is projected to rise, while American Tower, having achieved its leverage target, is poised to resume dividend growth.

feed.title: Why Stifel Sees IBM as a "Safe Compounder" in AI
feed.summary: Stifel analyst David Grossman reiterated a Buy rating on IBM with a $325.00 price target, viewing the company as a "safe compounder" in AI due to software-led growth and margin expansion potential. The firm anticipates organic software growth of 7-8% and sees service fundamentals improving. Stifel is maintaining its 2026 estimates for IBM, noting that the Confluent acquisition is expected to be modestly dilutive.

feed.title: OnTheMarket Says CoStar Backing Delivering Traffic, Stock And Leads Surge
feed.summary: OnTheMarket is reporting significant growth in traffic, inventory, and lead generation since its acquisition by CoStar Group in December 2023. The company, which aims to challenge the UK’s existing portal duopoly, states website visits are up 55%, page views 110%, and property stock 47%. This surge is attributed to CoStar's investment and expertise, reinforcing OnTheMarket's goal to become a leading portal in the UK.

feed.title: Plug Power Balances Sines Milestone With Funding Risks And Dilution Relief
feed.summary: Plug Power has achieved a significant milestone with the completion of a large electrolyzer installation at Galp's Sines Refinery in Europe, establishing itself as a key player in green hydrogen solutions. Concurrently, the company is addressing its capital structure through a shareholder meeting to vote on authorized shares and has removed a potential dilution risk by modifying its agreement with Walmart. These developments highlight Plug Power's dual focus on expanding its industrial-scale projects while managing financial stability and shareholder concerns.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Visa, Mastercard, and PayPal
feed.summary: This Zacks Analyst Blog highlights Visa's expansion in the digital payments ecosystem through a partnership with Mercuryo, enabling crypto-to-fiat off-ramping via Visa Direct. The article also touches upon how competitors Mastercard and PayPal are strengthening their positions in the digital payments space. It underscores Visa's strategy to integrate its infrastructure with emerging digital asset platforms and explores growth opportunities in cross-border crypto transactions.

feed.title: Does WEC’s Leadership Transition and Higher Dividend Payout Reshape Its Long‑Term Capital Allocation Story (WEC)?
feed.summary: WEC Energy Group is undergoing a leadership transition with Gale Klappa retiring and Scott Lauber taking over as chairman and CEO, signaling strategic continuity. The company also increased its quarterly dividend to US$0.9525 per share. These developments reinforce WEC's focus on consistent earnings, a strong dividend record, and disciplined capital allocation.

feed.title: Why Stifel Sees IBM as a “Safe Compounder” in AI
feed.summary: Stifel analyst David Grossman reiterated a Buy rating for International Business Machines Corporation (NYSE: IBM) with a $325.00 price target, viewing the company as a "safe compounder" in AI. This positive outlook is attributed to IBM's software-led growth, potential for margin expansion, and defensive characteristics, with the firm maintaining its 2026 estimates for revenue, margins, EPS, and FCF. While acknowledging investment risks, Stifel recommends IBM for defensive-minded investors, despite a potential modest dilution from the Confluent acquisition not yet reflected in estimates.

feed.title: Alta Wealth Advisors LLC Takes $1.37 Million Position in Leidos Holdings, Inc. $LDOS
feed.summary: Alta Wealth Advisors LLC has initiated a new position in Leidos Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:LDOS) by purchasing 7,226 shares valued at approximately $1.37 million during the third quarter. This move is part of broader institutional interest, with several other firms increasing their stakes in the aerospace company. Leidos recently acquired power design firm ENTRUST for $2.4 billion, a strategic move expected to bolster its energy/utility engineering capabilities and revenue growth.

feed.title: Uber (UBER) Stock; Falls Slightly as NYC Enforces 10% Tip Prompt
feed.summary: Uber's stock dipped slightly after New York City implemented a mandatory 10% tip prompt for food delivery apps, raising concerns among investors about its impact on revenue and operating costs. Uber, DoorDash, and Instacart are appealing the new regulation, which also increases the minimum wage for delivery workers. Investors are closely watching Uber's upcoming earnings report for insights into how these regulations will affect its financial performance and whether similar policies could spread to other cities.

feed.title: A Look At Boston Properties (BXP) Valuation After Recent Share Price Weakness
feed.summary: Boston Properties (BXP) has experienced recent share price weakness, with declines over the past month and three months, contrasting with a positive three-year return. Despite trading at US$64.77, below some estimated value measures, the article explores whether this presents a buying opportunity or reflects anticipated future growth. While one narrative suggests BXP is 18.5% undervalued with a fair value of $79.43 based on future office demand and strategic capital recycling, a Discounted Cash Flow model indicates an even higher fair value of US$97.

feed.title: Ross Stores Former Chairman Norman Ferber Passes Away
feed.summary: Norman Ferber, former Chairman and CEO of Ross Stores, passed away on January 23, 2026, at the age of 77. Ferber was instrumental in transforming Ross Stores from a small chain into a leading off-price retailer. The company and the Ross Stores Foundation have honored his memory through contributions to the Ross Cares Fund and the naming of an Art and Academic Room, reflecting his commitment to community and education.

feed.title: Micron Plans Nearly $24 Billion Singapore Investment To Address AI-Driven Demand
feed.summary: Micron Technology is investing nearly $24 billion in a new wafer fabrication plant in Singapore over the next ten years to meet the surging AI-driven demand for memory chips. This expansion includes creating significant cleanroom space and approximately 3,000 new jobs in Singapore. The investment aims to address the global memory chip shortage, with production expected to begin in late 2028.
